title: Release Gates, Migration, Recovery, Support, and Defense · Runtime Lens: Migration Compatibility
summary: The team ships from generated volume, closes no playtest barriers, cannot identify an artifact, and treats reinstall as the recovery plan. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the migration compatibility increment in production release, rollback, and residual-risk defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Compare behavior, saves, settings, key maps, artifacts, and accessibility across upgrades with rollback-safe migrations.
Require schema, order, duplication, source, model, algorithm, native GUI, accessibility, performance, security, package, and learner-flow gates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 1491 during a corrupted-state recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend release evidence, migrations, recovery rehearsals, support ownership, and residual risk; the prior good state must survive failure; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. A defensible solution applies Migration Compatibility, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to production release, rollback, and residual-risk defense.

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-migration-compatibility — Compare behavior, saves, settings, key maps, artifacts, and accessibility across upgrades with rollback-safe migrations.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-release-recovery-defense-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-release-recovery-defense-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_release_recovery_defense_2(release_gates: int = 12, passed_gates: int = 12, rehearsed_failures: int = 6, open_critical_barriers: int = 0, rollback_minutes: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if release_gates < 8 or passed_gates < 0:
        return False, passed_gates, "release-depth"
    if passed_gates != release_gates:
        return False, passed_gates, "failed-gate"
    if rehearsed_failures < 4 or open_critical_barriers > 0:
        return False, open_critical_barriers, "recovery-gap"
    if rollback_minutes < 0 or rollback_minutes > 15:
        return False, rollback_minutes, "rollback-budget"
    assert passed_gates == release_gates
    return True, passed_gates, "defensible-maze-release"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-migration-compatibility-iu8gr7
# Competency: maze-migration-compatibility.
# Case 6021: a corrupted-state recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it.
def evidence_maze_migration_compatibility_iu8gr7(release_gates: int = 12, passed_gates: int = 12, rehearsed_failures: int = 6, open_critical_barriers: int = 0, rollback_minutes: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_iu8gr7 = "iu8gr7-7rg8ui-6021"
    assert evidence_variant_iu8gr7
    evidence_axes_iu8gr7 = (
        "gridiu8gr7",
        "topology6021",
        "search7rg8ui",
        "eventiu8gr76021",
        "access6021iu8gr7",
        "recovery7rg8ui6021",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_iu8gr7)) == 6
    if release_gates < 8 or passed_gates < 0:
        return False, passed_gates, "release-depth"
    if passed_gates != release_gates:
        return False, passed_gates, "failed-gate"
    if rehearsed_failures < 4 or open_critical_barriers > 0:
        return False, open_critical_barriers, "recovery-gap"
    if rollback_minutes < 0 or rollback_minutes > 15:
        return False, rollback_minutes, "rollback-budget"
    assert passed_gates == release_gates
    return True, passed_gates, "defensible-maze-release"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Migration Compatibility model · Theory Migration Compa…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Migration Compatibility: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 9868 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend release evidence, migrations, recovery rehearsals, support ownership, and residual risk; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare behavior, saves, settings, key maps, artifacts, and accessibility across upgrades with rollback-safe migrations.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Migration Compatibility model · Theory Migration Compa….
steps.why: Retrieving Migration Compatibility through the concrete production release, rollback, and residual-risk defense case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Migration Compatibility model · Theory Migration Compa…, keep Migration Compatibility connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare behavior, saves, settings, key maps, artifacts, and accessibility across upgrades with rollback-safe migrations.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Migration Compatibility model · Theory Migration Compa…, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Migration Compatibility precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Migration Compatibility model · Theory Migration Compa…, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 9868 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend release evidence, migrations, recovery rehearsals, support ownership, and residual risk; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare behavior, saves, settings, key maps, artifacts, and accessibility across upgrades with rollback-safe migrations.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Migration Compatibility model · Theory Migration Compa…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A new version only needs to open its own newly created state.
